name: CSP INC /MA/
business: CSP Inc. develops and markets IT integration solutions, advanced security products, managed IT services, cloud services, purpose built network adapters, and high-performance cluster computer systems. It operates in two segments: Technology Solutions (value-added reseller of third-party hardware/software and IT services) and High Performance Products (cybersecurity software, network adapters, and legacy DSP products). The company serves commercial and defense customers worldwide.

exposures.sentence: The ARIA SDS solution is a software portfolio comprised of 3 products: ARIA Packet Intelligence software which is used by customers for its high-speed wire-rate in-line packet filtering, steering and policy enforcement functions. ARIA Advanced Detection and Response ( ADR ) solution used by customers to find and stop threats in real-time by monitoring their entire network, device and services footprint.

CSPi and its subsidiaries develop and market IT integration solutions, advanced security products, managed IT services, cloud services, purpose built network adapters, and high-performance cluster computer systems to meet the diverse requirements of our commercial and defense customers worldwide.

Myricom SmartNIC adapters ( ARC Series and Myricom Secure Intelligent Adapters or SIA ) are optimized for and sold into markets that require high-bandwidth and low-latency including (i) packet capture, (ii) financial transactions, (iii) machine vision and (iv) network security.

Myricom SmartNIC adapters ( ARC Series and Myricom Secure Intelligent Adapters or SIA ) are optimized for and sold into markets that require high-bandwidth and low-latency including (i) packet capture, (ii) financial transactions, (iii) machine vision and (iv) network security.
Cloud services that include Microsoft 365, Azure, Azure Virtual Desktop, Greencloud, Amazon Web Services and Google Cloud Platform.
We assist our clients with designing and implementing HCI solutions from multiple vendors including DellEMC, Nutanix, HPE and Cisco.

ARIA AZT PROTECT was designed to fill a gap in the market stopping the most sophisticated attacks that are used to attack critical infrastructure applications before harm can be done. It compliments other protection technologies in place and can stop some of the most well-known attacks including the SolarWinds attack, and the recent Russian sponsored Sandworm attacks used on utilities and energy infrastructure.
Manufacturing Market Our focus for fiscal year 2026 and beyond is to expand from our initial successes more broadly into this market and its various sub segments.

Myricom SmartNIC adapters ( ARC Series and Myricom Secure Intelligent Adapters or SIA ) are optimized for and sold into markets that require high-bandwidth and low-latency including (i) packet capture, (ii) financial transactions, (iii) machine vision and (iv) network security.
Our current customers are in web and infrastructure hosting, education, travel, telecommunications, healthcare services, distribution, financial services, professional services and manufacturing.
Our current customers are in web and infrastructure hosting, education, travel, telecommunications, healthcare services, distribution, financial services, professional services and manufacturing.

Third party products and professional services are marketed and sold through the Company s direct sales force into a variety of vertical markets, including automotive, defense, healthcare, education, federal, state and local government, and maritime.

catalysts.event: Introduction of ARIA Zero Trust (AZT) PROTECT to the market
catalysts.sentence: In July of 2023 ARIA Zero Trust (AZT) PROTECT was introduced to the market.

catalysts.event: Buy-in contract for UK defined benefit pension plan expected to transition to buy-out within fiscal year 2026
catalysts.sentence: In October 2024, in connection with the planned termination of our defined benefit pension plan in the U.K., we paid 8.5 million British pounds to enter into a buy-in contract. ... the expected timeframe of the buy-in contract turning into a buy-out contract is within fiscal year 2026.

catalysts.event: TS segment backlog expected to ship during fiscal year 2026
catalysts.sentence: The gross backlog of customer orders and contracts for the TS segment was approximately $7.1 million as of September 30, 2025 compared to $4.9 million as of September 30, 2024. It is expected that all the customer orders in backlog will ship and/or be provided during fiscal year 2026.

catalysts.event: HPP segment backlog expected to ship through fiscal year 2026
catalysts.sentence: The gross backlog of customer orders and contracts in the HPP segment was $1.0 million as of September 30, 2025 as compared to $0.8 million as of September 30, 2024. It is expected nearly all of the customer orders in backlog will ship and/or be provided through fiscal year 2026.
